heading: Repealed. , ,Pub. L. 91–606, title III, § 302(3)84 Stat. 1759Dec. 31, 1970
label: 1855aaa to 1855nnn Repealed. , ,Pub. L. 91–606, title III, § 302(3)84 Stat. 1759Dec. 31, 1970
notes: Section 1855aaa, , , , set out Congressional statement of policy in enacting .Pub. L. 91–79, § 183 Stat. 125Pub. L. 91–79Oct. 1, 1969
, , , set out as a note under section 1855aaa of the title, gave the name “Disaster Relief Act of 1969” to .Pub. L. 91–79, § 1683 Stat. 125Pub. L. 91–79Oct. 1, 1969

Section 1855bbb, , , , authorized the President to allocate funds for permanent repair and reconstruction of non-Federal streets, roads, and highway facilities destroyed or damaged as a result of a major disaster.Pub. L. 91–79, § 283 Stat. 126Oct. 1, 1969

Section 1855ccc, , , , covered allowable alterations in timber sales contracts between Secretary of Agriculture or Secretary of the Interior and a timber purchaser in event of a major disaster causing major physical changes.Pub. L. 91–79, § 383 Stat. 126Oct. 1, 1969
Section 1855ddd, , , , authorized Secretary of the Interior to give public land entrymen additional time to comply with requirements of law.Pub. L. 91–79, § 483 Stat. 126Oct. 1, 1969
Section 1855eee, , , , made provision for Small Business Administration disaster loans.Pub. L. 91–79, § 683 Stat. 127Oct. 1, 1969
Section 1855fff, , , , provided for emergency farm loans by Secretary of Agriculture.Pub. L. 91–79, § 783 Stat. 127Oct. 1, 1969

Section 1855ggg, , , , provided for development of State disaster relief programs, development of State agencies to administer disaster relief programs, and reports to Congress.Pub. L. 91–79, § 883 Stat. 128Oct. 1, 1969

Section 1855hhh, , , , made provision for appointment and duties of a Federal coordinating officer to operate under Office of Emergency Preparedness in any area designated a major disaster area.Pub. L. 91–79, § 983 Stat. 128Oct. 1, 1969

Section 1855iii, , , , authorized the President to provide on a temporary basis dwelling accommodations for individuals and families displaced by a major disaster.Pub. L. 91–79, § 1083 Stat. 128Oct. 1, 1969

Section 1855jjj, , , , authorized the President to set up a food stamp and surplus commodities program to distribute food to persons in low-income households unable to purchase food as result of a major disaster.Pub. L. 91–79, § 1183 Stat. 129Oct. 1, 1969
Section 1855kkk, , , , provided for unemployment assistance to persons unemployed as result of a major disaster.Pub. L. 91–79, § 1283 Stat. 129Oct. 1, 1969

Section 1855, , , , covered grants and loans for funds used in the suppression of fire on forest or grass lands which threatens destruction as to constitute a major disaster.lllPub. L. 91–79, § 1383 Stat. 129Oct. 1, 1969
Section 1855mmm, , , , authorized grants for removal of debris.Pub. L. 91–79, § 1483 Stat. 130Oct. 1, 1969
Section 1855nnn, , , , defined “major disaster” and provided termination date of sections 1855aaa et seq. of this title on .Pub. L. 91–79, § 1583 Stat. 130Oct. 1, 1969Dec. 31, 1970
For provisions relating to disaster relief, see section 5121 et seq. of this title.
Effective Date of RepealRepeal effective , see , set out as an Effective Date of 1970 Amendment note under , Internal Revenue Code.Dec. 31, 1970section 304 of Pub. L. 91–606section 165 of Title 26
